title: Factory Tutorial Part 6   Sequencers
description:
Factory is the new poly-synth from Sugar Bytes.

http://www.sugar-bytes.de/content/products/Factory/index.php?lang=en

00:24 Intro to the parameters
01:15 Curves and shapes
02:14 Sequencer as a mod source - hi-hats 
03:36 A kick drum
04:28 A bassline
06:03 A snare

transcript: [Music] [Applause] hello you're watching a series on sugar bites Factory my name's Tom km and today we're going to be focusing on the sequences we are going to be using the modulation Matrix quite a lot as well so if you're unfamiliar with how the modulation Matrix works there is a video to get you up to speed on the basics I I suggest you watch that first but if you're ready to go let's Jump Right In there's a sequencer tab down here it's next to the modulators and the articulation and we have four individual sequenc that we can control each sequencer has 16 steps we have a start point we have an end point and they also work as Loop points there is a onshop button so when this is enabled the sequence will stop on the last step we also have a negative values button that means that we can draw into the below zero values and go above zero if this is off everything is positive we have a clock divider that determines how long the steps last for and these can be different throughout the sequences and we also have a global swing which remains Universal throughout the sequences each sequencer can be used as a source in the modulation Matrix and I'm going to go ahead and make something for you in a second but first let's just cover these things down the right hand side there's a few more functions this one shifts everything to the right this one shifts everything to the left this one copies this one pastes this one randomizes and this one deletes now you'll notice when I did the randomization thing we get all these crazy shapes that's because these sequences absolutely rock with their shapes okay each single step you can do a really crazy different shape for each one we do have some standards like curves up we've got our curves down we can do uh Little Bell kind of curve things like this but we can do things like have two steps going up so this one has one step at 50% of the other one this is two steps per step we have triplets here so let's have triplets going up as well so 1 2 3 that's a triplet in one step we can do quods going down for example so you can get really detailed with your sequences um in in the sugar bites Factory scent it's really quite cool the other one I should mention is this one here if I click and drag oop excuse me if I click and drag this across so that it's covering all of these steps they're all the same it basically means we can make our own custom envelopes so this is really good if the adsi envelopes and the modulators aren't really doing it for you you can draw your own incredibly custom shapes with the uh I just call it the play function anyway let's make something so I'm going to go to sequence one I'm going to clear everything I'm going to bring my oscillator 1 down bring my oscillator 2 down and bring my white noise up this is the mixer volume of the noise so this is not automatable but this one is this is a volume as well so I'm going to bring this down to its lowest Val I'm going to choose sequence of one from one of my sources and I'm going to choose the noise level as my destination I'll then give it a maximum value and you'll see that we've got a blue line now halfway through the volume that's because we have a completely 50% dead line going through the middle of the sequence so I'm going to bring everything down I'm going to make sure we're playing 16th notes in the clock divider and I'm going to choose this shape so let me just throw let me just copy that shape across and we'll put in a few high hats and I'll play a note for you in a second let's make this one A Triplet up I really like that one where is it there it is so that will have a triplet up we'll make this one a double up we've also got these really interesting ones that have nothing at the start the first 50% is nothing and then it has a note so let's try one of those as well let's make this one a more of a slant down and actually let's make this one whole value and then we'll have a curve down from that very good now let's work on a kick drum I'm going to use the sub oscillator here so I'm bringing up the mixer volume for the sub again the automatable volume I'm bringing down I'm going to change the source of the sub to oscillator 1 and I'm going to set sequencer two to affect the sub level so like we did with the noise we're going to have sequence of two making a kick drum with the suble but I'm also going to apply sequence two to the sub pitch when I can find it pitch sub there we go maximum value maximum value and now if we go into sequence 2 we can start writing a kick drum I'm just going to delete everything here bring everything down choose that particular curve that we had before copy it across like so and now let's put in some kicks okay we're going at 132nd notes here so let's make this 16th notes very good let's add a Baseline okay oscillator 1 let's bring it up that's pretty boring so let's go ahead and use sequence of three to control the oscillator 1 level maximum amount of value and I'm going to go into my number three here drag the end point out and again I'm going to select this one it's quite good for single hits I'm going to bring it up drag it across so they're all the same and just throw in some random notes just rhythmical for now and we do need to bring the oscillator one a oh my apologies we need to be the oscillator one level we do need to bring this down so now we should get a kind of a one note rhythmic base not too bad but let's do something really freaky and use sequence of four to actually play different notes in the Baseline so I'm going to attach sequence of four to the OS one pitch or pitch one I'm going to give that maximum amount of value as well and now if we go into sequence of four uh again drag the Endo out we'll make this quarter notes long and then if I bring this up it should play a different note hopefully it's in key pretty cool we might as well add a cool kind of curve at the top there to make it go wild awesome now I wonder I might might as well try and make a sneer with this I've already used up all my sequences but what I think I can do is piggy back off the high hat so that was sequence number one what I might do is I might bring the levels down of all of these quite low and the sne are going to be where are they going to be they're going to be here so what I I'm going to do is make a slant down very loud on the where the sne should be should make this one a slant as well and I'm going to apply sequence one to oscillator 2 level I need to bring the the main volume fader up and what I think I need to do is choose something let's choose a what can we do let's try a wave table sync and I'll keep this Empire wave table what I'll do is I apply sequence one to oscillator 2 a and I'll also apply it to 2B let's see what happens might make some kind of snare sound I think I need to bring the tuning right down as well kind of piggy backing off that high heads let's maybe change the um wave table very cool and just for fun let's put a delay on it or something like that I'm actually quite happy with that so I managed to use all four sequences we made a drum a bass a kind of a sneer and some high hats that's the sequences really powerful stuff go ahead make some cool stuff my name's Tom km cheers
